summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Right knee pain following a trampoline fall.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports that he injured his right knee four days ago while jumping on a trampoline. He describes a popping sensation at the time of injury, followed by significant swelling. He took ibuprofen and applied ice, which provided some relief, but the knee remains difficult to move. He has been able to stand with some weight, but it hurts. He reports managing his diabetes with diet and occasionally forgetting to check his sugars.

Findings
Physical exam reveals significant swelling in the patient's right knee. Palpation elicits tenderness on the medial aspect of the patella. Range of motion is limited due to pain, with decreased flexion and extension. The McMurray's test is negative. Lung sounds are clear bilaterally. Heart sounds are normal with no murmurs. The patient reports difficulty with weight-bearing.

Assessment
The patient's presentation is most consistent with an injured medial collateral ligament (MCL) with associated swelling and pain. His diabetes management is also a concern.

Plan
I am placing the patient in a straight leg brace to immobilize the knee and reduce pain. I will prescribe a course of mobic for pain relief and to reduce inflammation. I will order an MRI to evaluate the extent of the MCL injury and rule out other potential damage. I will also order a hemoglobin A1c to assess his diabetes control. I will refill his metformin prescription to continue his current regimen.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Right knee pain.

MEDICAL HISTORY

The patient has a history of diabetes. She has been doing pretty good with her diet. She states that she forgets to check her sugars quite a bit.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Musculoskeletal: Reports right knee pain and swelling.

PHYSICAL EXAM

Respiratory
- Auscultation of Lungs: Clear bilaterally.

Cardiovascular
No murmurs, gallops.

Musculoskeletal
- Examination of the right knee: Some swelling present.
- Palpation: Some pain to palpation on the medial aspect of the right knee, and a little bit of pain on the lateral aspect of the right knee.
- Range of Motion: Limited range of motion as well as pain on both flexion and extension of the knee.
- Special Testing:
McMurray's Test: Negative.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Right knee pain.
- Medical Reasoning: I am concerned about a torn MCL due to pain on ambulation and trouble with weightbearing, as well as the pop she heard.
- Patient Education and Counseling: We discussed treatment options today including bracing, anti-inflammatories, and icing. - Medical Treatment: I am going to put her in a straight leg brace and I will prescribe some Mobic. She can start taking that as a pain reliever and to try to get some of the swelling down. I want her to ice her knee once an hour for about 15 minutes.
- Additional Testing: I am also going to send her out for an MRI.

2. Type 2 diabetes.
- Medical Reasoning: The patient states that her type 2 diabetes are well-managed.
- Medical Treatment: I am also going to get a refill on the metformin that she has been taking 500 mg.
- Additional Testing: We are going to recheck her hemoglobin A1c.

Patient Agreements: The patient understands and agrees with the recommended medical treatment plan.
